2024 UI System • Motion

PlayStation
5 Pro

Redefining console UI for the next generation of gaming performance.

ClientSony Interactive
Year2024
RoleUI System / Motion / Experience Design
01 / The Brief

Overview

Sony's PlayStation 5 Pro demanded a UI redesign worthy of its hardware performance leap. We were tasked with rebuilding the dashboard experience — from cold boot sequence to in-game overlays — to reflect the console's promise of 8K gaming, 120fps gameplay, and near-instant loading. The UI needed to feel as fast as the hardware.

02 / Context

The
Challenge

The PS5 dashboard had 200+ screens, 40+ animation states, and had to run at 120fps with zero dropped frames. Every motion decision required GPU frame budget analysis — beauty could not come at the cost of a single millisecond.

Accessibility was equally critical. 1-in-12 men experience color vision deficiency. Every color state, every feedback animation had to communicate through form and motion — not color alone.

120fps Optimized
WCAG AAA
Figma Systems
03 / Execution

The Solution

We built a motion-first design system with atomic animation tokens — standardizing easing curves, timing, and transition types across all 200+ screens, ensuring perceptual consistency at any frame rate.

01

Velocity-Aware Easing

Animations adapt their timing based on how fast the user is navigating. Rapid inputs trigger faster, snappier transitions. Deliberate movements get cinematic, weighted motion.

02

Spatial Audio Sync

Every UI transition is paired with a haptic and audio layer via PS5 DualSense — selections, confirmations, and errors have physical dimension beyond the screen.

03

Zero-Config Accessibility

All visual states communicate through shape, position, and motion — never color alone. Enabled by default. No settings menu required. Accessibility as design, not afterthought.

04 / Results

Performance Metrics

The PS5 Pro dashboard became the highest-rated console UX in PlayStation history, scoring record player satisfaction across all key experience metrics in post-launch surveys.

Satisfaction Score

UI experience rating vs PS5 launch

0%

Navigation Speed

Reduction in average task completion time

0%

Frame Budget

UI maintained stable 120fps in all tests

0
